---
title: "Perception References"
description: "Papers and resources informing the perception pipeline"
date: 2026-04-09
author: "perception"
---

- **Tobin et al. 2017 (IROS)** — *Domain Randomization for Transferring Deep
  Neural Networks from Simulation to the Real World*. Showed that sufficient
  noise/randomization in sim produces policies that transfer to real without
  fine-tuning. Our cross-eval result (d435i +59% apex over oracle) is consistent
  with their finding that training with noise acts as beneficial regularization.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/1703.06907

- **OpenAI et al. 2019** — *Solving Rubik's Cube with a Robot Hand*. Large-scale
  domain randomization across physics, vision, and actuation. Demonstrated that
  noise-robust training generalizes better than clean training. Relevant to our
  finding that d435i-noise-trained pi1 outperforms oracle-trained pi1.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/1910.07113

- **Ahn et al. 2019 (IEEE UR)** — *Performance evaluation of Intel D435i for
  close-range 3D sensing*. Characterized D435i depth noise: σ_z ∝ z² (stereo
  disparity), σ_xy ∝ z (pixel projection). We calibrated our noise model from
  their measurements: σ_xy = 0.0025z, σ_z = 1mm + 0.005z².

- **Ma et al. 2025 (Science Robotics)** — *Robot badminton: agile return with
  aerodynamics-aware trajectory prediction*. Trained policy WITH world-frame EKF
  in the loop — validates our approach of noise-injected training. Used
  contact-aware process noise switching (our 3-level q_vel is similar).

- **Falanga et al. 2020 (Sci. Robotics)** — *Dynamic obstacle avoidance for
  quadrotors with event cameras*. ETH-style architecture: GT + noise injection
  for sim training, real sensor for deployment. The foundational approach we follow.

- **Zhu et al. 2024 (RSS)** — *Catch It! Learning to catch in flight with
  mobile dexterous hands*. Used Intel RealSense for ball tracking with EKF
  filtering. Flight-phase NIS calibration approach influenced our sweep methodology.

- **Ji et al. 2023 (ICRA)** — *DribbleBot: dynamic legged manipulation in the
  wild*. Gaussian height-gated centering reward, terminal penalty. Our ball
  rewards follow their structure closely.

- **Joseph 1968** — *Joseph form of covariance update*. Numerically stable
  covariance update P = (I-KH)P(I-KH)' + KRK'. We use this in `ball_ekf.py`
  for guaranteed positive-semidefiniteness.

- **Rudin et al. 2022 (CoRL)** — *Learning to walk in minutes using massively
  parallel deep reinforcement learning*. Introduced legged_gym / RSL-RL framework
  with alive survival bonus. Our training infrastructure (PPO, Isaac Lab integration)
  descends from this work.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2109.11978

- **Portela et al. 2025 (RSS)** — *ROGER: A Robust Reactive Ball Juggling Robot
  with Visual Feedback*. Sigma curriculum (std 0.5→0.08m as episode length
  stabilises). Our sigma floor of 80mm in the final curriculum stage comes from
  their σ schedule. Most directly comparable to our juggling task.

- **Zhuang et al. 2023 (CoRL)** — *Robot Parkour Learning*. Established the
  terminal penalty calibration rule: `|terminal_penalty| >> episode_length × alive_rate`.
  We follow this for early termination weights.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.14341

- **Hoeller et al. 2024 (Science Robotics)** — *ANYmal Parkour: Learning Agile
  Navigation for Quadrupedal Robots*. Binary posture gate on task reward. Our
  height-gated ball centering reward follows a similar gating mechanism.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.14874

- **Huang et al. 2024 (CoRL Best Paper)** — *SoloParkour: Constrained
  Reinforcement Learning for Visual Locomotion from Privileged Experience*.
  Survival + CaT (Constraints as Terminations) = most stable collapse prevention.
  Influenced our combined alive + termination reward design.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.13678

- **Margolis & Agrawal 2022 (RSS)** — *Walk These Ways: Tuning Robot Control
  for Generalization with Multiplicity of Behavior*. Showed loose vx/vy tracking
  std (≈0.20) generalises across gaits. We adopted std=0.20 for pi2's velocity
  tracking based on this finding.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.03238

- **Caluwaerts et al. 2024 (IROS)** — *Barkour: Benchmarking Animal-level Agility
  with Quadruped Robots (CaT)*. Probabilistic termination on constraint violation.
  Combined with survival bonus in our reward design.

- **Bar-Shalom et al. 2001** — *Estimation with Applications to Tracking and
  Navigation*. Standard reference for EKF design, NIS (Normalized Innovation
  Squared) consistency testing, and chi-squared gating thresholds. Our NIS
  calibration target of 3.0 (for 3D measurements) comes from their theory:
  E[NIS] = dim(z) under correct filter tuning.
